signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 annual CPI inflation rose to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's March 2026 annual CPI inflation rose to 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% in February), undershooting market forecasts of 2.3-2.4% due to government fuel caps offsetting oil price surges.**[1][2][3]

signals.headline: Details:** This data, released around April 2, 2026 by Statistics Korea, confirms contained inflation pressures amid Mid
signals.raw: Details:** This data, released around April 2, 2026 by Statistics Korea, confirms contained inflation pressures amid Middle East-driven oil shocks, with monthly CPI at +0.3% (below 0.6% expected) and core CPI dipping to 2.2% YoY.[1][2][3] Bank of Korea held rates at 2.5% recently, adopting a wait-and-see stance for its April meeting, while forecasting inflation at 2.2% for 2026.[2][4] Prediction m
